description: Test cases for the slack-gif-creator skill. Create/compose/optimize/validate workflows evaluated via GIF frame inspection (LLM judge views sampled frames). Validate workflows additionally check stdout output.

test_cases.name: Bounce — ball falling with bounce_out easing
test_cases.prompt: Make me a Slack emoji GIF of a red ball bouncing up and down. Use smooth easing so it accelerates falling and decelerates near the top. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF (output.gif). A red circle moves vertically — it falls and slows toward the top, then falls again. Bounce easing is visible (not linear). Background is white or light. Animation loops.

test_cases.name: Pulse — heartbeat scale animation
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF of a red heart that pulses rhythmically like a heartbeat — it expands and contracts repeatedly. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. A red shape (heart or circle) rhythmically grows and shrinks. The size oscillates visibly across frames. Animation loops continuously. Background is clean.

test_cases.name: Spin — rotating star on dark gradient background
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF of a yellow 5-pointed star rotating on a dark blue gradient background. Use draw_star from core.frame_composer and create_gradient_background. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. A yellow 5-pointed star rotates continuously. Background is dark blue or a visible gradient. Animation loops smoothly. The rotation direction is consistent across frames.

test_cases.name: Fade — text fading in and out on colored background
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF where the word 'YES!' fades in then fades out repeatedly on a green background. Use RGBA alpha blending for the fade. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. 'YES!' text is visible at full opacity mid-animation, and invisible at the start/end of each cycle. Background is green. The fade transition is gradual, not a sudden cut.

test_cases.name: Slide — shape sliding in with ease_out easing
test_cases.prompt: Make a Slack emoji GIF where a green circle slides in from the left edge of the frame and decelerates to rest at the center. Use interpolate() with easing='ease_out'. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. A green circle enters from the left edge and smoothly slows down to stop at or near the center. The deceleration (ease_out) is visible — it moves fast at first and slow at the end.

test_cases.name: Particle burst — explosion radiating outward
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF of a colorful particle explosion: particles burst outward from the center in many directions and fade as they move to the edges. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. Multiple small colored dots/shapes radiate outward from the center of the frame. Particles spread toward the edges. The burst effect is clearly visible across frames. Background is dark.

test_cases.name: Zoom — circle zooming in from tiny to full
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF where a blue circle starts tiny at the center and zooms in to fill most of the frame. Make the zoom feel dramatic. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. A blue circle starts very small and grows frame-by-frame until it fills most of the 128x128 area. The zoom is noticeably dramatic and the size change is large.

test_cases.name: Loading spinner — rotating dots with trailing fade
test_cases.prompt: Make a Slack emoji GIF of a loading spinner: 8 dots arranged in a circle that appear to rotate, with trailing dots dimmer than the leading dot. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. Multiple small circles are arranged around a center point. They appear to rotate (like a classic loading indicator). Some dots are brighter and others dimmer, creating a tail/trail effect. Loops continuously.

test_cases.name: Progress bar — horizontal fill on message-sized GIF
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack message GIF (480x480, not emoji size) of a progress bar that fills from 0% to 100% left-to-right. Show a percentage number that updates as the bar fills. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 480x480 animated GIF. A horizontal bar starts empty and progressively fills to the right. A percentage number (0 to 100) is visible and increases as the bar fills. Animation ends at full/100%.

test_cases.name: Snowfall — particles falling with varying speeds
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF of snowflakes (small white circles or dots) falling from the top of the frame at different speeds and sizes. Background should be dark blue. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. Multiple small white or light-colored dots descend from the top of the frame. Particles move at different speeds. Background is dark blue or near-black. Scene looks like falling snow.

test_cases.name: Create then validate — emoji GIF passes Slack checks
test_cases.prompt: Create a simple 128x128 animated Slack emoji GIF (pulsing blue circle, 15fps, 12 frames). Then validate it using validate_gif from core.validators with is_emoji=True and verbose=True. Print the full validation report. Save the GIF as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A 128x128 animated GIF (output.gif) is created and exists on disk. validate_gif is called and prints a report showing: dimensions (128x128), file size in KB, frame count, fps, duration. The 'passes' result is True. Report is printed to stdout.

test_cases.name: Optimize — compare file sizes with and without deduplication
test_cases.prompt: Create a 128x128 animated GIF of a slowly pulsing circle (30 frames, 15fps). Save it twice: once as original.gif (num_colors=128) and once as optimized.gif (num_colors=48, remove_duplicates=True). Print the file size of each in KB and state which is larger.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Two GIF files are created: original.gif and optimized.gif. Both are 128x128. File sizes in KB are printed for each. The optimized version (48 colors, duplicates removed) should be smaller. Output clearly names both files and their sizes.

test_cases.name: Compose — planet orbiting the sun
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF of a small blue planet orbiting a larger yellow sun centered in the frame. The planet should complete one full circular orbit. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. A large yellow circle sits at the center (sun). A smaller blue circle moves in a visible circular orbit around it. The orbital path is clearly circular. Animation loops one or more full orbits.

test_cases.name: Compose — clock face with rotating second hand
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF of a clock face. Draw a circle for the clock, static hour and minute hands, and a red second hand that sweeps around the clock. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. A circle (clock face) is drawn. A red line (second hand) rotates clockwise from center across frames. Static hour and minute hands are also drawn. The result looks like a ticking clock.

test_cases.name: Compose — star with orbiting sparkles
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF of a central gold star with small sparkle dots that randomly appear and fade around it. Layer the sparkles on top of the star each frame. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. A yellow/gold 5-pointed star is visible at the center. Small bright dots appear at varying random positions around the star and fade or blink across frames. The overall effect looks like a twinkling/sparkling star.

test_cases.name: Compose — back_out overshoot slide-in
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF where a purple rectangle slides in from the right using back_out easing — it should visibly overshoot the center and then snap back to rest there. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. A purple rectangle enters from the right. It moves past the center of the frame (overshoot is visible), then moves back to rest at center. The overshoot and snap-back motion is clearly visible and distinct from a simple ease_out.

test_cases.name: Edge — single frame GIF (no animation)
test_cases.prompt: Create a valid 128x128 GIF with exactly 1 frame: a smiley face (two eye circles and a curved mouth arc) on a yellow background. It doesn't need to animate. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a valid 128x128 GIF file with 1 frame. A smiley face is drawn on a yellow background: two dark circles for eyes and an arc for a smile are all visible. File opens without errors despite being a single-frame GIF.

test_cases.name: Edge — high FPS at maximum 30fps
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF of a spinning star at 30fps with exactly 30 frames (1 second). Initialize GIFBuilder with fps=30. After saving, print the fps and frame count from the returned info dict. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF with 30 frames. GIFBuilder is initialized with fps=30. The save() info dict is printed, showing fps=30 and frame_count=30 (or close to 30 after deduplication). Animation is smooth due to high frame rate.

test_cases.name: Edge — three effects simultaneously: bounce + fade + spin
test_cases.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF that applies three animation effects at the same time: a yellow star that bounces vertically, fades in-and-out using RGBA, AND rotates continuously — all simultaneously in each frame. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a 128x128 animated GIF. A star shape shows all three effects at once: position changes vertically (bounce), opacity changes across frames (fade), and the shape rotates. All three effects are visible simultaneously. The animation is visually complex.

test_cases.name: Edge — 3-frame blink at slow FPS
test_cases.prompt: Create a minimal Slack emoji GIF with exactly 3 frames at 2fps: frame 1 fills solid red, frame 2 fills solid white, frame 3 fills solid red again. This creates a slow blink. Save as output.gif.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a valid 128x128 GIF with 3 frames. Frame 1 is solid red, frame 2 is solid white, frame 3 is solid red. At 2fps each frame is visible for ~0.5 seconds. The blink alternation between red and white is the only content. File loops.
